name: King Cash Pawn & Jewelry
description_short: King Cash Pawn & Jewelry operates eight locations across South Florida, offering pawn loans, item sales, and buy-now-pay-later financing with rates starting at 3% monthly interest.
description_long:
King Cash Pawn & Jewelry is a regional pawn shop chain based in South Florida with eight operating locations. The company positions itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional lending, emphasizing friendly and respectful customer service with multilingual staff (English, Spanish, and Creole). Founded on the pawn business model, the company has expanded into adjacent financial services to serve customers seeking quick cash or alternative credit options.

The company's core service is collateral-based pawn lending, where customers receive loans against personal items including gold, diamonds, watches, musical instruments, electronics, and tools. King Cash advertises paying 95% of gold value and offers free in-person and online appraisals. Beyond pawning, they offer direct purchase of unwanted items for cash and have launched a buy-now-pay-later financing product offering 0% interest for up to 6 months on purchases. Pawn loans have a minimum 61-day repayment period with no maximum, starting at 3% monthly interest (36% APR) depending on loan amount, with the option to extend by paying accrued interest. The company provides a mobile app (MobilePawn) for remote payment and pawn management.

Key differentiators include the company's emphasis on customer service quality, multilingual capabilities, and relatively straightforward pawn terms compared to some competitors. They advertise an A+ rating and prominently feature their "friendliest pawn shop" positioning. The eight-location footprint provides regional accessibility across South Florida. Their buy-now-pay-later product with 0% promotional rates offers an alternative to high-interest small credit products.

The primary caveat is that pawn lending remains a high-cost borrowing option: even at the stated 3% monthly rate (36% APR), the annual cost significantly exceeds traditional personal loans or credit options. The 61-day minimum term means short-term borrowers face substantial fees. While the company appears professionally operated, pawn shops inherently target consumers with limited access to traditional credit, making them a last-resort option rather than a primary financing solution.
pros: Advertises paying 95% of gold value, competitive for precious metals
Offers free appraisals both online and in-person
Multilingual service in English, Spanish, and Creole
Mobile app (MobilePawn) allows remote payments and pawn management
Eight South Florida locations provide regional accessibility
Buy-now-pay-later product offers 0% interest for up to 6 months (with restrictions)
No maximum repayment period for pawn loans—borrowers can extend indefinitely
cons: Starting interest rate of 3% monthly (36% APR) is substantially higher than traditional personal loans or credit products
Minimum 61-day pawn term ensures short-term borrowers incur significant fees even for brief loans
Finance product carries restrictions not fully disclosed on main website; financing approval contingent on employment and bank account verification
Collateral-based model means borrowers risk losing personal possessions if unable to repay within term
Limited geographic footprint (South Florida only) restricts access for out-of-region customers
best_for: South Florida residents needing immediate cash for personal items they own and can spare
Customers with valuable gold, diamonds, or watches seeking quick liquidity without sales pressure
Unbanked or underbanked consumers with limited access to traditional lending who need short-term emergency funds
Businesses or individuals looking to liquidate used electronics, tools, or musical instruments quickly
diagnosis: King Cash Pawn & Jewelry is best for South Florida residents with valuable personal items who need immediate cash and have limited access to traditional credit; the primary caveat is that pawn lending at 3% monthly interest (36% APR) remains substantially more expensive than conventional personal loans and should only be considered for genuine short-term emergencies.
services: Pawn loans against gold, diamonds, watches, musical instruments, electronics, and tools
Direct purchase and sale of unwanted items for cash
Buy-now-pay-later financing (0% interest up to 6 months with restrictions)
Pawn loan extensions by paying accrued interest
Mobile app (MobilePawn) for payment management and pawn renewal
